name: Aedwulf
title: The Banemantle
bio: Dwarven migrants are always valued in Archlands for their hardiness and craftsmanship. Their descendants are stalwart supporters of the crown, though often indifferent to the Order. After the fall of Ice-Eagle House, Aedwulf's heroic ancestor Ogain Ax-Lover brought his kin south and established a forge. Aedwulf grew up working that forge, learning diligence and responsibility, traits that served him well when he joined the queen's army with dreams of earning renown.
Yet, Aedwulf was only a competent fighter. Each day he rose, renewed his oaths, and trained. Each night he went to sleep disappointed.

Prior to her denunciation, the Chapter sent Renna's most loyal units far away, so Aedwulf found himself marching into the craggy northern range. There, he felt his Ice-Eagle blood, and his own mediocrity, most keenly, along with something else — a pull toward reckless, selfish thoughts. The first caresses of the Void's seduction.

That any of the unit survived the expedition was due to the sacrifice of the company's most steadfast soldier. While his comrades fled a horde of Void thralls, Aedwulf planted himself where the path shot through a tight defile. When the horde's Host appeared, Aedwulf summoned his courage, opened himself to the Taint, and brought the walls of the fissure down around them.

Aedwulf was not rewarded with the heroic death he sought. Instead, he was entombed alongside his foe, the Host sustaining his lifeforce only to feed on Aedwulf's Thoughts. Endlessly reciting his oaths, he resisted the Void for years. But no one resists the Void forever. In that lightless prison, the Host slowly consumed him… until a Stone-rage shook the mountains.

While Aedwulf had held on to some fraction of his mind, his body had been all but conquered by the Void. Returning to his homeland, Aedwulf steeled himself for a new battle: protecting Archlands from himself.

name: Angelo
title: Artist Extraordinaire
bio: Cassandra claims she bought Angelo from a disgraced nobleman for threepence. He cannot refute her for he has been among the troupe from the time of his earliest memories, and none of them go back that far. He acts, sings, dances, even does comedy. Performance is not only his trade, it is his passion! He basks in the adoration of loving fans and obsesses over how to ensure their continued acclaim. The desire to play more heroic roles drove him to become an expert fencer by seeking out swordmasters to tutor him as the troupe traveled the Archlands.

With Angelo, it is difficult to tell where the act stops and the man begins. Sometimes he wondered if he was, in fact, Angelo or some amalgamation of all the parts he had played. This crisis of identity even led to Angelo creating a second persona, a part unlike any other, for it is one not confined to the stage: the people's protector, the Threepenny Prince! Thereafter, the Prince stalked towns the troupe visited in search of wrongdoers. Enthusiasm for the role often got the better of him and the Prince's valor exceeded good sense.

Once, thieves lured him into a trap, but a mighty woman intervened. Later, they had drinks; then they had each other; then she disappeared. Asking around, Angelo heard about an anchor-throwing girl and convinced Cassandra to recruit her. He assumed they would resume their tryst, but Rosie had no memory of him nor was she affected by his charms. Angelo's unwelcome attentions ultimately drove her to leave. Cassandra warned him against it, but Angelo snuck off after Rosie anyway, hearing she'd taken up with Void Hunters. But what better company for a hero such as the Threepenny Prince?

name: Arbias
title: The Expurgator
bio: Orphaned and deemed too slow-witted to become a warrior or cleric, Arbias spent his entire youth sweeping the halls of the Arx Lucis. Tomas, the future High Questorian, recruited Arbias into his personal service, molding him into a fighter and informant. Throughout Tomas's rise, Arbias was ever at his heels.

Later, Arbias would join Tomas's cadre of expurgators for the excision of Void-corrupted Order personnel. Jealous, Arbias did not trust his fellow "Salamanders". Justifying his suspicion as being in the interest of his master's and the Order's safety, he began to keep tabs on the other Salamanders.

It was weeks before he managed to slip in unseen and follow one of them deep below the Arx Lucis. He watched as the Salamander whispered blasphemous words to a locked door. Runes inscribed upon the lock glowed purple… Void sorcery! With a cry of triumph, Arbias ambushed and killed his comrade, just as the door opened.

In the chamber beyond, a young girl sat meditating. Behind her stood a kind of upright sarcophagus. She beheld Arbias, bloody blade in hand. A scream made him clutch his head in pain, though her mouth had not opened, and searing radiance filled his vision.

He awoke imprisoned by Salamanders. His eyes seemed unharmed. More, they possessed new sight, for he could see, to his horror, that all of the Salamanders, including himself, were Tainted! With desperation's strength, Arbias broke free and fled.

Void Hunters found him, wounded from encounters with the Salamanders, who had hunted Arbias since his escape. The masks he claimed hung from his girdle and he had tied a rosary around his eyes. Raving, Arbias begged for help in saving his master from secret Void monsters. He was clearly mad, but most Hunters are a little mad and many are Tainted. Anyone who could take down four Salamanders was welcome.

name: Ashei
title: The Endless
bio: When she was young, Ashei's mentor Yoari inundated her with tales of the power shamans wielded before the dry times, awakening in Ashei a lust to command that ancient strength. At fifteen, she journeyed into forbidden lands in search of the means to do so. There, she came into contact with the Void and, believing it to be a state beyond life and death, she voluntarily embraced the Taint.

After returning to her home village, Ashei exhibited sorcery far beyond Yoari's. Her former mentor immediately stepped down, but Ashei no longer had any interest in the social responsibilities being shaman entailed. Instead, she used her position to learn about any legends or rumors that might empower her further.

Thus did Ashei hear of the Eel of the Grasses, a legendary serpent of enormous size which lived deep in the dreaming plains. Many hunters from her tribe were lost from hunting those lands, and the survivors were reluctant to speak of them. An excitement like never before came over Ashei. She set out to find the monster and, using her Tainted gifts, to make its might her own. She followed the survivors' tales as far as she could, then she followed the remains of the serpent's victims all the way to its lair.

When the monster attacked, Ashei let herself be swallowed whole … but it was Ashei who devoured the serpent, merging with it from the inside, gaining its physical powers and predator instincts, warping and enhancing them with the Void's foul sorceries. What finally emerged from the years-long metamorphosis was an immortal Consumed of frightful strength. Gifts of the Void are ever double-edged, however. The new being, despite its might, was eternally hungry for more. This hunger persists even after being Reclaimed by the Hunters, who call her Ashei the Endless, in acknowledgment of her insatiable desire for greater power.

name: Bohdan
title: The Lone Wolf
bio: Lands in the prosperous Crownlands have often been the reward for exemplary service. So it was with Bohdan's family. His ancestors had fought in all of the kingdom's wars. But Bohdan took his family seat in a time of relative peace. With no war to fight, he spent his time complaining there were no opportunities to demonstrate his prowess, which he said exceeded his predecessors.

Fate provided, in the form of a major Void incursion. Bohdan fought heroically but he had been lax with his soldiers and vassals, who shared his reckless disregard for proper training. Seven in ten of his warriors died alongside most of his knights.

The Queen stripped Bohdan of his lands, now divided between another lord and the lingering Void. His pride undiminished, Bohdan complained that he had been unjustly punished for the failure of his vassals and levies, "It was their duty to be ready when I called, not my duty to mold them into what the call required." A lord's sole responsibility, he claimed, was to inspire through valorous feats of arms. Had he not performed this duty exceptionally well?

Bohdan had not survived the battle entirely unscathed, for he lost the use of his left arm. He often boasts he is still, even one-handed, more than a match for any other knight. He tells anyone who listens he would have a retinue if he wanted to be dragged down by "unreliable" hangers-on.

Twin fires burn in him now: his ire toward the feckless Queen for stripping him of his hereditary honors and, far hotter, hatred of the Void for taking everything else. It was these grudges that resonated so strongly when he heard the Hunters' call.

name: Bomor
title: The Mighty
bio: Long before he was a Hunter, Bomor had earned renown among the Free Tribes, felling a mastodon with his bare hands and making its skull his weapon. What Bomor had in strength, though, he lacked in wisdom. He challenged the chieftain — his older sister — to a duel for leadership of their tribe and was soundly beaten. She allowed Bomor to tire himself out with strong attacks and let his ferocity lead him into vulnerable positions. "You are mighty, Bomor," she told him, "but victory requires cunning, too. There you are sorely wanting. Come back when you have learned this lesson."

Bomor was cast out of the tribe but his shame drove him still further away to Archlands, where he became a mercenary. Often, he worked alongside Hunters, and sensed he could learn much from Leo and Nadia, despite his childishness and her cynicism. For their comrade, Twist, he never cared.

Bomor was driven more by his desire for growth than by the battle to end the Void. Though he pursued the endless war with as much commitment as his fellows, he was usually the one that reminded the others there was more to life than vying with the Void, even for a Void Hunter.

Bomor has changed much since his early days on the plains. Though he speaks little, what he says is usually wise counsel. He no longer grandstands. His battles are quick and to the point. He is no less fierce, but strength is no longer his only tool. Once he wore skulls to intimidate foes, now he keeps only those of enemies and fallen allies from whom he learned valuable lessons. Sometimes, his fellow Hunters will come upon Bomor conversing with the skull of T'lo, taking long pulls on his pipe and listening as though the long-dead mastodon were imparting wisdom from beyond death.

name: Bruno
title: The Balladeer
bio: A history of hardships has taught the secretive dwarven clans who live beneath the hills abutting the western edge of Moonlight Forest caution. Children like Bruno were rarely allowed to play above ground, and only on condition that they remain quiet and never enter the wood. Bruno was afraid as any other of the darkness between the trees, but he was even more curious. Sometimes he thought he heard music drifting out from beneath the mysterious canopy. One day, he could no longer fight the urge to seek its source. He was not seen again for almost a year.

When Bruno suddenly emerged from the forest, it was clear he was changed. He spoke of following "the singers" for days that felt like years. Sometimes they had left him morsels and sometimes their voices diverted aggressive beasts. Sometimes they abandoned him, but they always returned, just beyond seeing. He began to add his voice to theirs, for he sensed in their strange attentions the desire to teach. Finally, the singers led him home.
Bruno's new, expressive personality could no longer be contained in the Halls-beneath-the-hills. Quietly, he departed and, singing all the while, he walked until he crossed paths with the Carnivale.

Before long, he was known as the troupe's most beloved spinner of tall tales. Only Cassandra believed his fantastical tales of the forest were mostly true, if strongly embellished. All delighted in his magical voice, though, for the way he could tug upon the heart as well as the spleen, drawing tears and mirth with equal skill. Many years Bruno spent happily wandering the realm with his found family, until the rising threat of the Void made such a lifestyle too dangerous and too hard to earn a living.

name: Cassandra
title: Madame Fate
bio: Given their marginal status, the realm's few wealthy elves have good reason to be anxious about maintaining appearances. Unfortunately, Cassandra's vitiligo bore some resemblance to Void corruption, so it was out of misplaced love and protectiveness for their daughter — as well as fear and shame for their reputation — that her parents kept her sequestered away from the world throughout her youth. To alleviate her loneliness, the girl performed card tricks and fortune tellings for her dolls and the servants. Yet these dramatizations were not mere play.

Cassandra often heard strange melodies in alien languages, though no one else could hear them. She tried to ignore them. She couldn't. She told them to go away. They didn't. Finally, Cassandra stopped trying to screen out the words. She listened, and found they were, impossibly, messages about future events, though Cassandra struggled to make sense of them.

One day, in an eerie voice, Cassandra told her mother her next trading excursion would bring fortune and misfortune in equal measure. Her mother went anyway, making a mint, but perishing on the road home. Cassandra's father blamed his "accursed" daughter and banished her. Cassandra slept in gutters, pilfering to survive until she was chased into the wilderness.

She wandered until she heard another melody, less strange but reminiscent of those in her head. It led her to the Carnivale's camp, and a new family. The nomads' life suited her. Cassandra honed her talents and persona as a fortune teller. Eventually, she became the head of the nomadic family – protective, like her parents, but warmer and more accepting.

As Void outbreaks became more common, the Carnival was often barred from entering towns. With no coin to be had, the troupe disbanded. This time, Cassandra did not wander, for the melodies had told her to seek out Void Hunters, whose fate she knew was intertwined with that of the whole world.

name: Eleazar
title: The Abomination
bio: Eleazar was "born" in the pits beneath the musty laboratories of Dulur, where flesh from countless failed experiments is slowly rendered by chemical processes into colorless organic clay. He had been a human assistant to a group of Korpskraft Meisters trying to create new fighters, grafting venom glands, poison sacs, and neurotoxin-producing nematocysts onto subjects both willing and compelled. Every attempt failed, the grafted hosts unequipped to regulate the toxins coursing through their bodies. Eleazar believed he could succeed where they failed. To that end, he began conducting secret experiments of his own.

When the Meisters discovered this, they were wroth. How dare a human presume to challenge or, worse yet, improve upon Digir Korpskraft! How dare he succeed! They attempted to replicate his findings, using Eleazar himself as a subject. An acidic pleurobranchus capable of absorbing and dissolving prey through its skin was grafted onto him. But they did not understand his techniques, and they failed.

Eleazar was thrown into the rendering pit, presumed dead. However, the slug attached to him began absorbing toxin-producing organs. Rather than destroy him, the volatile chemicals counteracted one another, somehow revitalizing him. Confused, he erupted into the lab, killed several Digir, and escaped.

When he encountered a group of Void Hunters, they nearly mistook him for a Void Host. Eleazar remembered nothing before the pit nor of how he could mix formulas to regulate his patchwork body. In the meantime, he sought battle to hone his abilities, gaining respect among the Hunters, but also among the Sentinels sent by the Korpskraft Meisters to reclaim him. Few have survived to report their failure to Dulur.

But there is one who dogs him still – an independent contractor who fights like a Sentinel without limitations. Eleazar will end her, too, and anything standing between him and his creators. They will all pay for his ceaseless torment, as his body remains a battleground of pain every waking moment.

name: Faustus
title: The Unchurched
bio: In any other time, Faustus would have been considered his generation's leading light — a prodigy risen from the humblest origins to the highest echelons of the Order; a conceptual genius whose Dualist philosophy inspired numerous theological treatises; an Invocator Adeptus who had burned to cinders some of the strongest Void Hosts encountered in living memory. None of it had come easily. All Faustus achieved had come by working harder than everyone around him. Thus, when he met Viorath, the virtuoso for whom every dazzling feat seemed effortless and every revolutionary insight seemed an afterthought, they were instant rivals.

Blasius Luca had taken an interest in the contentious philosophers to fill the empty position in the Chapter. Each spent hours in private interviews with him. Often, Luca's interest focused on the Void: Could it be defeated? Could it be wielded for the Light?

Faustus bristled at the questions. "To embrace the Void is to invite Taint into one's soul," he said, emboldened by conviction. "Could one wield it? Certainly. Should they? Never." The chamber door to opened. "Our faith is a war," Luca said as Viorath entered, "and it needs warriors to defend it." Faustus looked on while his rival was commanded to demonstrate Luca's point with Faustus's blood.

The battle that ensued would have been spoken of for ages had anyone witnessed it. In the end, Viorath proved stronger — barely. Faustus used the last of strength to distract his opponent with an illusory double while he escaped.

The Chapter, which now included Viorath, immediately declared Faustus and his views to be anathema. Anyone with knowledge of his whereabouts would be greatly rewarded. Anyone extolling his now-forbidden Dualism would be executed. Now, Faustus actively seeks allies opposing the Order, offering his might to help bring down the Chapter.

name: Galen
title: The Sage
bio: As a boy, Galen feared his father's temper. He spent most of his time wandering the deep forests near his home where, during high tide, the sea overflowed the river so that the tall pines rose straight out of the water. There he encountered ancient ruins made of strange stones that seemed to respond to his presence. Galen took some of them home and he found, if he focused his mind, he could make them move as he willed. Disapproving of his son "playing with rocks", his ignorant father demanded Galen accompany him to sea.

One day, Galen realized what looked like an island was actually more ruins, submerged bythe sea, but his father refused to approach. Galen became upset. More stones rose from beneath them, splitting the hull of their small boat. He woke upon the shore, but he never saw his father again.

Returning home meant being shackled to the place for good — he would take up his father's trade to provide for his mother. The secret of the stones would remain forever a mystery. He chose not to return to that fate. Instead, he journeyed far and long, learning much of the stones, the ruins, and the civilization that built them.

At first it felt like nothing but paranoia — suspicious figures glimpsed in dark taverns or behind him on empty roads. Then folk in towns he passed through told him that dangerous-looking men had been asking about him. Galen turned his considerable intellect to divining the identity of his shadows, but years passed during which he learned little: a name, some symbols, a vague plan he could never allow to come to fruition. He is a solitary man now, waging a war of the mind with an unseen opponent and, in all the world, only he knows what is at stake.

name: Goran
title: The Paineater
bio: Goran learned the world's truths on the streets, fending for his life against other toughs, struggling to keep he and his little brother fed after their parents died. Sometimes in the ruthless world of Ainon, though, it seems the only way to survive is at the expense of others. He fought to become boss of his own gang, the Brickeaters, taking by force and giving to his brother all the comforts they had lacked. Goran's violent methods earned him many enemies, however, and despite the security he thought he'd bought them, he was unable to protect his brother from the reprisals of rival gangs.

Goran's brother clung to life after suffering severe injuries. Only the chirurgeons and healing invocators of the Order could have saved him. Goran pleaded for the priests to help, but his reputation was known and they demanded a price he could not pay. He was desperate, though, watching his brother slip away, so when cowled men from a secretive cult offered him another, darker path, he took it. Goran traded his freedom for his brother's health, suffering years under the Shadow Imperative's ministrations.

In captivity, Goran was submitted to torturous rites of dark sorcery. All that kept him going was the belief that his brother was alive. As he became more familiar with the cult's evil ways, though, he assumed that his brother must be held in similar straits. One day, he privately swore, Goran would be strong enough to rescue him.

His desire for strength deepened the Void's influence until Goran was Tainted. Using the Void's power, he escaped, massacring his tormentors. Afterward, he searched for his brother in vain until the Void Hunters offered him aid. In exchange, they asked only that he fight and suffer — the two things he had done all his miserable life.

name: Jagya
title: The Huntress
bio: On her twelfth birthday, Jagya's father, kennel-master to a marcher lord, entrusted her with a puppy named "Brighteyes" to raise and train. Jagya was determined to impress her father and Brighteyes responded to her dedication, learning quickly and demonstrating that he would never slink from any danger which threatened Jagya.

Two years later, Void attacked the manor. Jagya tried to get to her father, but Brighteyes tackled her. The building she had been trying to reach collapsed; the hound had saved her life. Together, they fled, and when they returned a few days later, the manor was empty but for corpses.

The manor was no longer safe, so they had to make their way in the Wilds. Jagya trained herself to use the crossbow with deadly accuracy. She disguised herself with trophies from her kills, appearing increasingly like the Voidbeasts she hunted. Brighteyes, meanwhile, grew large and fierce. Jagya trained him to corner prey and to lure Voidspawn into ambush, but unplanned encounters were inevitable.

In close-quarters fights, Brighteyes proved the bane of many Voidspawn. But discolorations appeared in the hound's eyes and his fur fell out in clumps. Brighteyes was gradually being Consumed – a process about which Jagya knew tragically little. She decided they must seek a healer in the Heartlands.

The Void presence had waxed since the manor attack. They traveled cautiously. After many days they reached a village. When Jagya approached, her outlandish garb caused alarm. Brighteyes sprang to her defense, and the villagers saw that he was Tainted.

The pair took to living in the Wilds again. Jagya sought rumored cures for the Taint, but only the Void Hunters she met told her anything of substance. Looking at the pair admiringly they told her, "There's no cure, girl, but join us and we can promise neither of you will live to become monsters."

name: Johannes
title: Consumed Heart
bio: Johannes was once among the most skilled human Korpskraftsmen in Dulur. In the city where every race was nominally equal, a human practicer of the art, no matter how good, could only ever dream of attaining a truly prestigious position. However, associating with one of the Lateborn was quite the fashionable act of rebellion among the young, anti-establishment elves. With their support, Johannes found his place among the Acolytes. His intellectual discussions with his unruly peers gradually shifted his focus from Korpskraft to other, darker areas of research.

In time, Johannes became wholly devoted to the idea of inverting the properties of the Void; to make of it a productive battery rather than an engine of consumption. Driven by the obsession, he became more and more reclusive, until his only companion was the half-elf Tigris. Johannes only tolerated her company because she'd promised to get him into the abandoned High City.

She failed — not entirely unexpectedly — and Johannes attempted to make the passage across the chasm alone, following the Urgis tunnels deep below the ice. There, he found ancient and eldritch structures comprised of unique stones which, he theorized, could delay Consumption.

Johannes constructed a device to harness this capacity but, unbeknownst to him, his mind was already frayed by the Void, causing him to miscalibrate. An implosion drew parts of his body into the vortex powering his arcane machine which, instead of slowing down the Void's influence, accelerated it exponentially, consuming him in a matter of days. Only his defeat by Hunters ended his terror. There was one among them whose scholarly nature reminded Johannes of the man he once had been. And although that man is long gone, so is the Void's domination over his heart and mind.

name: Kurgan
title: House Ram Fury
bio: The House of the Mountain Ram has one creed: never yield. Conceding ground or granting quarter warrants shame. This makes them terrible conversationalists, but fierce combatants. Kurgan was the paragon of this ideology, earning himself many karls and a good marriage. But Kurgan's companion Spiri envied Kurgan and challenged him to holmgang. Kurgan won the duel, but could not bring himself to end his best friend's life. Instead, he said, "We saved each the other's life many a time, Spiri, but if I ever see you north of the mountains again, I'll smite ye so hard it'll add a 'T' to your name. Now, git!"

Spiri was as stubborn a dwarf as he was an envious one, though, and he did not leave immediately. He returned to their House and let it be known that Kurgan had granted Spiri mercy. Kurgan's shame was great. His wife was taken from him. Thane-granted gifts were stripped from him and he was treated thereafter like a common ceorl. A fury was kindled in Kurgan then, and he swore that he would enact a vengeance on Spiri Rygstikker such as the North had never seen.

Spiri was as cowardly a dwarf as he was stubborn and envious, though, and he fled. Kurgan set off after him but it was many years before he caught up to him. By then, Spiri had been Consumed and transformed into a powerful Host, but Kurgan's wrath had led to Taint of his own. He, too, drew on the Void to match his foe's new strength. Still, alone, even empowered, he could not defeat Spiri, who could summon armies of Consumed. Kurgan could think of only one group who would fight beside one Tainted and shamed as he. He traveled south in search of Void Hunters.
Coming soon...

name: Lamassu
title: Eternal Vigil
bio: Throughout their long experience, especially since encountering the Void, the Dark Elves of Ekurion and Dulur have lost much of their history and culture, and no place contained more of it than the lost Cathedral of Learning. Before the fall of the High City, the Digir have secured their knowledge in the fortress-like bibliotheca, defended by gargoyles animated by the souls of those interred in the Necropolis. Through their dark technics, the ancient Seelkraft Meisters allowed the gargoyles will limited strictly to protecting what lay within the silent halls. Sadly, the Cathedral eventually fell to the Void, its knowledge and the gargoyles' sole animating purpose lost with it.

Some gargoyles were destroyed defending the Cathedral and some, with nothing to protect, reverted to inert stone. Others, however, were Consumed by the Void, and these lost not their vitality but only the limitations their makers placed upon their will. One particular gargoyle ripped itself from the stone pediment on which it had been intended to spend eternity and now roams the northern regions of the Archlands, spreading Void corruption in its wake like a pestilent wind.

Some remnant of the creature's original guardian consciousness remains, though, for it often appears when weaker Consumed are under attack. Some who are aware of the strange, dark champion believe it has come to think of the Void as in need of its protection. Few have offered alternative theories, but many have raised the question: what might become of it if ever Reclaimed from the Void? Would it become inanimate statuary once more, like so many of its fellows? Or would transfer its protective nature onto those that resist the Void? In the event, the Hunters who finally freed Vigilus from the Void found themselves the beneficiaries of its guardian consciousness.

name: Lanthe
title: Eyes of the Forest
bio: Legends say, in the deepest shade of the Moonlight Forest, there is a mirror-like pool wherein Moon is said to hide when Sun, her husband, walks the sky. In truth, she who dwells in the lake is not Moon, but Lanthe, the first Sylvan Seer grown by the Wood in centuries. She is the personification and expression of the Wood's hopes, fears, and desire for action. Beyond the forest's edge, the Void is rising, and the Wood has called forth its avatar to take part in the war that fast approaches.

When Lanthe emerged from her chrysalis, fully formed and blazing with the Forest's vitality, she declared that the Moonlight Clan must end its long isolation. Only a bare few heeded her. When she warned that change was coming, most ignored her. Change is always coming, they said, no matter what the Clan does. Why take action now? When she lamented the elves had forgotten their role as protectors and mentors to other races, all dismissed her. How could the Clan protect the Lateborn from themselves? Weeping emerald tears, Lanthe chose to seek less complacent allies.

Immersing herself in the pool at the heart of the Forest, Lanthe became one with its prodigious consciousness and was able project her Thoughts far beyond the borders of both her body and even those of the Moonlight Forest. Something called to her, resonating with a memory of the ancient Wood, of an elder race opposed to the Void. Following it, Lanthe found the Void Hunters and now her spirit battles beside them, though her physical body still resides in the pool. However, to fight she must step from the spirit into the physical world, and in that brief span she is as vulnerable as any of her companions.

name: Leo
title: Butcher of the Void
bio: Leo's first memories are of the Order orphanage in which he was raised. Resources at the orphanage were deliberately scarce, and the boys often scrapped, unless they could discourage a challenge, leading Leo to develop his swagger. He often ran away, but was always caught in the act. Father Carmine, the only priest who genuinely cared about the boys, gave him a key to the tunnels below the orphanage. "I know you'll make your escape eventually, Leo," he told the lad, "but if you ever need or want to come back, the door will be open." Leo used the key to run away several more times, but always followed his grumbling belly back to the orphanage.

Recruiters for the crown's army often visited the orphanage, offering meager pay but regular meals, so Leo became a soldier. He was periodically promoted for valor only to be demoted for insubordination. Despite his worst efforts, he earned a cushy assignment as captain of the garrison positioned to monitor shipments coming out of the Crucible.

Then Leo met Twist, who enticed him to use his position not to prevent, but to effectuate smuggling. The young men became friendly rivals, gloating over the other's failings while also urging each other to greater feats of daring.

Eventually they were discovered and had to seek opportunities elsewhere. It was Twist's idea to become Void Hunters, but it was Leo who took to it with the most gusto. The life of a Void Hunter gave Leo everything he had ever wanted - excitement, freedom, coin, and adoration. Leo's complicated feelings toward Twist underlay his keeping Nadia, Twist's sometimes lover, at arm's distance, since he knew it was only a matter of time before Twist tired of her affections. These tensions eventually led to a fight and then a more-or-less amicable break between Leo and his old partner.

name: Lilith
title: Dark Apothecary
bio: Mistrusted by society, labeled "witch" and "heretic", Lilith was nonetheless consulted when villagers needed help the Church couldn't — or wouldn't — provide. Once, plague came to an ill-fortuned town and its people were all but abandoned by the Order's healers, who seemed all-too-ready to declare the settlement a lost cause. The villagers turned to Lilith. She observed that people survived at a rate much higher than she expected. Investigation revealed that the herbs she had gathered to treat them had been Tainted by the remains of Consumed buried deep beneath their roots.

Lilith continued experimenting, splicing Tainted plants with uncorrupted ones. Her concoctions were incredibly effective, saving almost half of the plague-cursed town. The survivors told others and Lilith's reputation grew until even members of the nobility would sometimes darken her doorstep. But, while very effective in the short term, even Lilith could not know the effect her Void-enhanced curatives would have over time. Eventually, it became obvious that those she administered were more likely to become corrupted by the Void. Soon, the community was calling for blood.

Lilith had a scientific, pragmatic mind. She was well-prepared for this eventuality. She quickly vacated her home, bringing only what she needed to continue her experiments. There was simply no way she was abandoning her pursuit for safer and more effective restoratives enhanced by Void-tainted ingredients.

Lilith sought out Void Hunters, who she suspected would prove more "open-minded" subjects. After all, what is a little Void-exposure to such as they? Many Hunters are already Tainted, or even Consumed, though, and she worries her results might be skewed. She's begun to consider "treating" the populace once more — a consideration she has not shared with her new comrades.
Coming soon...

name: Lysadorea
title: Sister Peregrine
bio: Lysadorea was born in Orandi Village, after Saint Hermeida cleansed it of the Void and before it fell to Order perfidy and neglect. From a very young age, Lysa displayed deep faith in the Light, moderate magical abilities, and a significant — sometimes alarming — interest in the life and legacy of the Saint. She was selected by the prelate to become a Devotrix, a nun chosen for her devoutness, healing abilities, and fair appearance, groomed to accept rich petitioners who came to receive blessings and healing. As a Devotrix, Lysadorea lived a cloistered life, until she encountered the poor and needy on a pilgrimage to the Grand Cathedral.

The experience added a sense of urgency to her piety, and when she returned to Orandi she undertook a forty-day fast, during which she received a vision of Saint Hermeida. On the fortieth day, she informed the prelate she would take up the way of the Germani Itinerati, a sect of migratory healers originally founded to honor Saint Hermeida, which had died out when moving about the Kingdom became too dangerous. The hierarchs forbade it. In protest, Lysa continued to fast until she was naught but skin and bones. Her visions intensified.

The Church realized they must acquiesce or have a dead Devotrix on their hands. Moreover, she was beginning to wield some influence with the other initiates, who started spreading rumors of the girl beyond the cathedral's walls. Finally, the prelate relented and endorsed her visions and her new mission.

Lysadorea took the name of Sister Peregrine, and she walked the realm until her feet were ragged. Frequently during her travels, Void Hunters have saved her from bandits and Consumed, and she has often healed their wounds. The respect she has for them challenges the church's insinuations that they are all enemies of the Light.

name: Nadia
title: The Faithless
bio: Nadia and her younger sister were both inducted into Order military training after their parents, nobles outspoken against the Church, were hung as heretics without public trial. An intelligent youth with a tendency toward skepticism absorbed from her parents, Nadia never believed the Order's accusations, nor was she convinced by their obviously manufactured "evidence".

Nadia hated the Church, and was resentful and insubordinate toward her superiors, for which she was flogged, isolated, and forced to go without rations countless times. Nadia endured, considering the best plan for deserting with her younger sister when her she was old enough.

By the final year of her apprenticeship, Nadia was growing frustrated with her sister Natalie's fervent adoption of the Church's teachings. She knew that if she was going to pry her sister away from the Order, she needed to act soon. She often snuck out of the barracks at night to visit taverns, hoping to make contacts that would help them on the outside. That was how Nadia met Twist and Leo and formulated a plan for her and Natalie to become Hunters. Natalie would have none of it, though. Instead, she raised the alarm and delayed Nadia so that the older sister actually had to kill a paladin in order to escape.

Since becoming a Hunter, Nadia has adopted a lot of Twist's irreverence, but like Leo believes the mission of the Hunters is a worthwhile one, not just a way to quick coin. She hates the Order more than ever for besmirching her parents' names and turning her only sibling against her, but she has learned much more about the Order's hypocrisies. Now she has no pity for those like Natalie who have allowed themselves to become, as she sees it, "brainwashed" zealots. What she cares about now is taking care of those few she trusts and others persecuted by ecclesiastical "justice".

name: Natalie
title: Aegis of the Light
bio: Natalie was made paladin apprentice along with her sister when their parents were executed on charges of heresy and treason. While her older sibling disbelieved the accusations, Natalie trusted the judgment of the Church. She considered her service in the Order to be a kind of interdiction, clearing their family name and maybe even absolving her parents' sin in the next world. She committed herself to her training wholeheartedly, rising in esteem despite the vitriol and outright hatred directed at her by peers and superiors who saw only the daughter of traitors. Even during her early training, she earned a reputation for piety, toughness, and effective leadership.

Apprenticeship was not easy for Natalie. Her parents' shame, not to mention association with her misbehaving older sister, bred condescension and hostility from superiors. Natalie was forced to endure much more than the rest of her cohort, even physical and mental abuse. All the while, she told herself that the Light was testing her, and the moment she became a paladin, later a commander, it would all be proven to have been worth the hardship and sacrifice. Sadly, she was unable to bring Nadia to see things similarly.

When Nadia attempted to "rescue" Natalie from her apprenticeship, such was her commitment to the Order that Natalie did not hesitate to report her sister's plan to desert. Doing so resulted in the death of a senior paladin and her estrangement from her sister. Natalie believes she did the right thing, but part of her wonders if she was too ready to step over her sister to gain greater respect in the Light's judgment. She wonders if she could have done more to renew Nadia's faith, to save her from the fate of their parents. She does her duty now with the motivation to never see another family shattered by the Void's corruption.

name: Nathaniel
title: The Righteous
bio: The defining moment of Nathaniel's early life came while performing altar duties in a small town in the Kingdom's hinterlands. Though he was only a low ranking acolyte, he learned and was successful in exposing the fact that the church's priest was Void-Tainted.

Nathaniel was lauded as a "scourge of evil", attention which naturally led the boy to develop an inflated view of his own importance. Claiming that he had been visited by an angel during his private prayers, he announced that he would assume the priest's duties. His predecessor's pyre was a dramatic backdrop for the boy's first sermon.

The townspeople were doubtful when Nathaniel, still a youth of fourteen, took over the priest's pulpit, but he refused to relinquish it. Whatever else was true, he became an inspiring orator, able to make the faithful weep or profess glory unto Light with a cunning turn of phrase. Before long, his congregation was growing, and people were calling him "father" in the streets. Inevitably, the small town became too small for his great purpose, so he journeyed to the capital. Nathaniel quickly surmised that the ecclesiarches there lacked sufficient zeal. He demanded a pulpit, with a growing flock of adherents at his back, and began preaching his radical views.

Nathaniel's sermonizing challenged key points of Order dogma and, more problematically, he accused many of his superiors of abusing their station. This could not be tolerated by the elites. Paladins soon barred him from entering any Church. When he was officially anathematized, his followers abandoned him and he became nothing more than an itinerant madman.

But madmen are common among the Void Hunters, and when they did not immediately shun him, Nathaniel came to view them as worthy targets for evangelism — genuine enemies of the Void, not mere pretenders like most others.

name: Owyn
title: The Folk Champion
bio: Owyn's peasant family had little time left for sermons after sowing, harvesting, repairing defenses, and fending off Void attacks. Their traditions were those of rustic folk — the kind that differ from town to town, though they preserve love and gratitude toward the Light. Later, he was indifferent toward Order doctrine — a common attitude for soldiers who often came face-to-face with people near as monstrous as the twisted minions of the Void. Owyn's life changed on a battlefield fighting against rebels who had revolted following the dethronement of Queen Renna.

Owyn had been wounded unto death. His belly opened and entrails spilled by a bearded ax. He went to his knees, desperately trying and failing to stuff his innards back where they belonged. An Order healer could do nought for him but read a prayer for the safe passage of his soul to the world of Light. When the cleric had gone, Owyn prayed too, promising the God of Light that he would live to serve its glory, fighting the only battle that mattered, protecting all people against the onslaught of the Void. And as life poured out of him, Light poured in.

A column of perfect radiance descended upon him from above like a rod of rigid lightning. Excruciating heat coursed through him, but when it passed, he was healed. Since then, peasants, soldiers, and even preachers see Owyn as a saint. But while it is true that he now possesses miraculous resilience, he has never felt particularly blessed. A promise is a promise, though, so now Owyn only fights on the frontlines against the Void, neither encouraging nor discouraging the rumors about him. He's realized that protecting people from the Void also means protecting them from the hopelessness it spreads.

name: Raven
title: The Unbroken
bio: First and principally I, Raven, Baroness of Ravensmoor, being of whole mind, commit and commend my untainted soul unto the hands of the Light my maker and preserver. And my Tainted remains to purifying fire, that I may never return to harrow the lands I and my ancestors so loved in life. I am the last of those that vowed to remove the Void from Ravensmoor, and though I fight on I know that my end must be near. My soul aches to be once more with all whom I have lost, yet will I die with regret, having failed to reclaim this benighted land.

Second, I hereby decree that any subject of Ravensmoor able to produce proof of former occupancy will have their property returned to them when, Light willing, it be safe to do so. Further, any descendant of my own Blackwing knights – heroes all who fought beside me and, to the last, died for my cause – shall be granted estates from my own domain and elevated to lordship.

Lastly I appoint my son, Sir Tancred of the Blazing Scions, to be my sole heir and lord of Ravensmoor upon my demise, but not to take up that title, its rights, or privileges until and unless he should purge that land of the Void. Tancred acts the lord and condemns me like as though he were a priest. Let him rescue these lands, and thereby substantiate that which he has long averred: that his mother was unworthy long before she was Tainted, and that he deserves dominion over what she has sacrificed everything to reclaim – eating ash among ghosts while he has played at being a hero in the queen's retinue. Let him bleed for his land, as I have.

name: Reed
title: Shadow's Edge
bio: When their traveling troupe of performers was accused of thievery in a rural town, Reed's parents and a few other non-humans from the troupe were left behind to bear the consequences. Reed became "apprentice" to another performer, the knife-thrower Kitzler, though all he ever taught his ward was how to move through the crowd and cut purses — that and all the slurs for "elf". Eventually, Reed was caught and Kitzler refused to pay for his release. Reed wept, feeling alone in the world — orphaned, abandoned, despised. They were the last tears he would ever shed.

A corrupt guardsman sold the boy to a gang of thieves. The "work" suited him, but he felt little love for the other members of the gang, humans and bigots all. They never gave him an equal cut, nor let him move up in their ranks, and they called him "knife-ear" behind his back. Eventually, Reed had enough. He told the authorities about a major heist the gang was planning. The soldiers ambushed the thieves and, during the chaos, Rreed managed to make off with a small fortune.

Months later, a tavernkeeper he owed offered to wipe his debt if Reed would kill the man's rival. Thereafter, he made his way as an assassin, even joining the Crimson Vipers, though he refused to kill other elves. He made his own list of targets — people, like Kitzler, who liked to make trouble for non-humans. Reed pursued his vengeance a little too successfully, though, and ended up with a bounty on his head.

Evading pursuit, he ran into a group of Void Hunters battling Void. Reed helped them and, when his pursuers caught up, the Hunters returned the favor. He decided to stick with them until the heat was off, but his list is still growing…

name: Renna
title: Queen Unsilenced
bio: The story of Renna's ascension to the throne and subsequent fall is not only her personal tragedy but the realm's. From the day she was crowned queen, Renna was as proactive and capable as her mother had been decadent and ineffective. Though a devotee of the Light, Renna was determined to restore to the crown the dignity, respect, and power the Church had long since diminished.

Renna actively toured the Crownlands — her shield and mentor Bernard by her side — inviting scholars, engineers, and philosophers to her court. She was feared by some, respected by all, and the people began to hope she might restore the strength of the realm, which had been waning for years.

Renna's vigorous leadership was taxing, especially after the birth of her daughter Sarah. During the Southern War, Renna chose to command personally, relying on medicines administered by Order priests to maintain her stamina. Her successful campaign bolstered her popularity, emboldening her to enact broad reforms throughout the Archlands. However, the Chapter challenged her reforms, and she banished their representatives from her court. The relationship between Church and Crown disintegrated precipitously thereafter.

Viorath himself accused the Queen of Void corruption. During her trial, signs of her Taint were displayed and many of her supporters abandoned her, even steadfast Sir Bernard. Renna cursed the Chapter, accusing Viorath's minions of exposing her to the Void through their medicines. Her accusations fell on deaf ears.

Supreme Sentencer Argenta spoke for the judges, naming her "abomination", stripping her of the crown, and commanding she be raised in a deadman's cage, dangling from her former castle's battlements as a lesson to all. Renna turned to the only asset remaining to her and drew Void power through her Taint to escape. Now, she seeks allies to help her exact revenge.

name: Robin
title: The Spitfire
bio: Robin grew up amongst the Hunters in Holdfast but, like most in the remote community, she was  born elsewhere, in Thousand Lakes, a region routinely beset by orc raiders. Robin's mother died, burned alive in their once-happy home during such a raid. The Order's warriors arrived too late to prevent hers and many other deaths, so Robin nurses an intense animosity toward the Church in general. She and her father, who became Holdfast's huntmaster, left to escape painful memories.

Robin became a crackshot with her bow, often beating Void Hunters in contests. Her father tried to discourage her from adopting some of the Hunters' more colorful figures of speech, but she still became known for her mouth as much as her aim. Spending time with the irreverent Hunters made her lighthearted and quick with a joke, but since Holdfast was sacked and her father killed — dying in flames just like her mother — she often broods, rage smoldering just below the surface.

Her ability to manipulate fire and use it in battle is the manifestation of her own inner, burning rage, represented as the same flames that have twice scorched her happiness to cinders. Combining this new gift with her marksmanship, she has learned to infuse her arrows with sorcerous fire, but she wonders if this might be only a fraction of what she is truly capable of. For the girl who always supped on the Hunters' tales, these abilities and the opportunity to take part in those adventures is a heady brew — even her recent tragedies cannot totally dampen the thrill she feels as she embarks upon this new journey.

name: Rosie
title: Brawny Belle
bio: Before she was Cassandra's Brawny Belle, Rosie was already famous. People gathered on the docks where she worked every day to see her lift several heavy crates over her head at once. Rosie's size was matched by her appetites, though, and it was the cost of satisfying them that made it necessary for her to bolster her wages with more performative feats of strength. Cheering audiences — growing almost as fast as her debts to the local taverns — gathered to see her throw anchors and down ale from buckets.

Ale was not her only vice. Rosie loved a good rumble and never missed a chance for a scrap. One night, stumbling out of the tavern she'd just been ejected from, she saw some fool in a mask cornered by alley cutpurses. She jumped in and, she assumes, saved him. There might have been something after but she's forgotten, spontaneous romantic dalliances being yet another one of her ill-advised indulgences. Eventually, the consequences of her rebellious lifestyle came back to bite her.

With all the battered ruffians, spurned lovers, admirers, and debt collectors getting in the way at the docks, losing her job was inevitable. Luckily, the fortune teller Cassandra had heard of the anchor-tossing girl and offered her a spot with her troupe. The Carnivale shared Rosie's zest for life and together they were happy as pigs in mud.

Angelo's infatuation, born in the heady aftermath of Rosie's intervention with the alley cutpurses, was a problem. Adding to that, hard times meant audiences weren't as free with their coin. Rosie quit, but soon had second thoughts and returned, only to find the camp ransacked by Void and the troupe nowhere in sight. Assuming the worst, Rosie wants payback. Becoming a Hunter seems the best way to get it.

name: Ruck
title: The Artisan
bio: Born to parents in the upper echelons of dwarven nobility, Ruck had the privilege to spend much of his young life gazing up at statues of artificers in the Hall of Splendour. That he possessed the genius and skill necessary to one day see his own likeness beside them was taken for granted by all, not least by Ruck himself. Master Artificer Buri Hammersoul chose the young prodigy to be his student.

Hammersoul forged new artificers the way he forged steel, sparing no sentiment for hurt feelings. Ruck had little to fear, but the other apprentices were constantly insulted and severely punished. The difference in status caused a rift between Ruck and his fellows.

Ruck attempted to earn their friendship by refining their work, but Hammersoul could easily perceive the difference in workmanship. He responded by punishing everyone except Ruck, and afterward the young genius was outright hated by his fellow apprentices.

Resentful, Ruck's self-control snapped. He no longer held himself back, humbling others at every opportunity, even pointing out Hammersoul's mistakes. This was a bridge too far; the Master Artificer dismissed Ruck. His goals now beyond reach, Ruck set out for Archlands without protest, though he vowed not to let himself act so recklessly in future.

Ruck easily established a reputation for fine work and innovative, beautiful design and amassed a small fortune. He enjoyed the freedom of living under the open sky, and designed a mobile workshop so that he could travel freely. Once, he was set upon by roaming Void beasts. Only the timely intervention of Void Hunters, some of whom were Tainted, saved him. In the battle, he witnessed Void used to combat Void.

Thereafter Ruck became driven by the desire to apply that experience to his craft. Unlike his previous innovations, only Hunters embraced these, thankful for the edge it granted them in battle. In their gratitude, Ruck finally found the gratitude the warmth he never felt beneath the fiery mountain.

name: Severin
title: The Execurator
bio: Severin grew up in one of the orphanages the Church use to develop desperate youths into dogmatic servants. Small in size, he was unable to stop the other boys abusing him. He was sure he was doomed to suffer all his days. Little did he know Hortulanus Lupercal, the orphanage's administrator, saw in Severin a potential asset. For years, Lupercal observed as Severin withdrew into himself, stealing away to private corners to dismember small animals — always with precise incisions. When the boy turned twelve, Lupercal trained the boy in the arts of torture and execution and made Severin his personal execurator.

The act of cutting flesh was the only thing that gave him a sense of control in a world that was too stressful for him to healthily cope. Severin's ministrations often went too far, resulting in unreliable confessions or the premature deaths of those under interrogation. He was punished, which only led to a greater need to cut. He began taking victims off the street, trying and failing to keep it secret. When Hortulanus Lupercal found out, he decided his execurator was a liability and sent some of his underlings to kill him. Severin sensed danger and survived the attempt, killing Lupercal's men in the process.

During the battle, Severin's mask was split, his face wounded. It was the first time in years he'd seen his own blood, and it was Tainted. One irrational hope drives him now: developing the Excidendi Immaculata, the "Perfect Excision" that could cut away even the Void. But it is not really the Taint he wishes he could excise; it is all his painful memories.

Lately, he has continued to indulge his compulsive behavior, but he has begun to consider joining the Hunters. Hunting offers plenty of opportunity to refine his cutting technique and, more importantly, they don't ask personal questions.

name: Sir Bernard
title: Her Majesty's Keep
bio: Young Bernard grew up in a noble family with deeply ingrained chivalric traditions and lay piety, their oaths to the crown of Archlands balanced by their humility beneath the Light. Gifted in fencing and horsemanship, Bernard's talent and imposing height helped him rise to become an unrivaled tournament champion. His prowess caught the eye of  Queen Malissa and, with a different lance in mind than the one he jousted with, she made him her personal bodyguard. But the proud knight's honor never faltered, and she soon tired of her him, naming Bernard the princess's protector instead.

Bernard, who had lost a child to the Purple Pox, was entirely devoted to the princess. When adolescent Renna desired to learn of war, he proudly taught all he could. After Queen Malissa's death, the 23-year-old Renna ascended the throne, with Bernard remaining her loyal knight. He earned the nickname "Her Majesty's Keep" after defending her from orc ambushers during the War in the South, felling no less than twenty enemy fighters and preserving the young queen until reinforcements could be recalled from the frontlines.

Yet even Bernard's stalwart commitment was strained by Queen Renna's rivalry with the High Chapter of the Holy Order. The steadfast Sir Bernard stepped aside when the Order exposed her Taint and raised her in a gibbet for all to profane. Now he cannot rest easily. As her teacher and companion, were her failings not also his own? Tormented by inner conflict, he wandered, his armor rusting from neglect. Struggling with his shattered world, Bernard enlisted in various campaigns, unable to escape his own tormenting guilt. Finally, desiring only a fitting death battling the enemies of the Light, he became a Void Hunter. His world was overturned once again when he learned that Renna had escaped condemnation and now keeps company with the Hunters, too...

name: St. Hermeida
title: The Blessed
bio: After the dark years of the civil war, some had the audacity to believe Light would illuminate the years ahead. They formed a new community, the Orandi, dedicated to healing the blackened corners of the realm through prayer. The Orandi sought out the Void's corruption, erected their tabernacles, and prayed until it receded. Early successes made them bold, but they were doomed. Sir Grendel Ravensmantle and his Blackwing Knights rode to their aid, but they arrived too late. All the Orandi had been killed or Consumed. All, that is, but one: the child who would become a saint, Hermeida the Immaculate.

The Chapter of that time wanted to use her. Instead, Hermeida chastened them for worldly ambition. She blazed like a second sun and the chapter abased themselves. Where Hermeida went, fallow fields became fertile and corrupted streams ran clear. With her Light alone, she cleansed the site of the Orandi's destruction. Thousands walked in her train, but none were more convinced of her blessedness than Hermeida herself. When she announced she would cleanse the Void wastes themselves, her most devoted begged her to reconsider. "Where I would go," she said, turning her back on them, "Doubters cannot follow."

It is believed she perished in battle with the Void, but this is not true. Hermeida lives, conquering and reconquering mere parcels of the wastes for the Light, only for the Void to return as she moves on. She is so focused on her inner Light, ever burning like a forge in the night, she is oblivious to the shadow without. She is both pure and Consumed, a kernel of undiminished Light shrouded in Void; two natures made more intense by their proximity to one another. In form, she is entirely Consumed, but her mind is consumed in a different way - deluded into believing that she is the manifestation of the Goddess of Light.

name: Tiche
title: The Undefeated
bio: Tiche was a dancing prodigy at Crown City's most prestigious academy until she was seen by Sir Lucelle, famed swordmaster and last of the storied Aurellian Knights. The old knight donated funds for construction of a new wing of the academy and the schoolmasters gratefully signed Tiche's apprenticeship over to him. Lucelle's creed was based not on faith, like Order knights, but on commitment to a personal code. Tiche dutifully learned all that he could teach her of honor, chivalry, and swordplay, at which she excelled even beyond Lucelle's expectations.

Her training was grueling, at times punishing. Tiche was forced to spar blindfolded to sharpen her focus and heighten her other senses. Tiche was a genius for mixing styles and even creating new ones. Lucelle resisted these tendencies, whether from dogmatic adherence to tradition or fear she would surpass him.

Tension between them grew until he decided to "demonstrate" his style's superiority and the importance of obedience. Tiche tried to calm him but instead had to fight for her life while unbridled rage overcame her master. He attacked, and Tiche saw the Void's long-metastasizing influence manifest in his eyes.

Lucelle pushed Tiche to her limit but, when she began to falter, his mind cleared. His hand hesitated so briefly no one else would have noticed, but in that hairsbreadth Tiche delivered a swift thrust through his heart — even as her own broke forever.

Now a living legend, Tiche always blindfolds herself in battle for, when she saw the Taint in her master's eyes, she surmised they must be the portals through which the Void infects the mind. Standing above his body, she pledged to always pursue mental as well as martial perfection; to honor but also to surpass the legacy of her master. As a Hunter, she adheres to that pledge every day.

name: Tombs
title: Hallowed Keeper
bio: A highwayman's club once determined the course that the life of a decent man would take. Due to his injuries, the man could not speak nor perform complicated tasks. His son despised the father for what he saw as the elder's weakness. After the son left, the man's wife could not work and take care of her husband at the same time. She beseeched the Church to take him into their care, to find some rewarding task for him.The priests made him caretaker of their rarely visited cemetery, said to be haunted by the children who died during the Years of Ice. The caretaker made dolls out of wood and grasses, and placed them on their anonymous graves.

When the Void assaulted the town years later, the caretaker did not flee with the other residents. Instead, he defended the resting places of the children until his last breath. The very ground shuddered beneath the weight of the attacking Void horde. The tremors disturbed the tombs and the mausoleum of a forgotten hero — locked through all the years of the caretaker's service — lay open before him. He entered, and emerged wearing the hero's armor and bearing the hero's arms. True to his purpose, he died in defense of his beloved children.

Though unsung, his heroism was not unwitnessed. The wayward echos of the children's consciousness gathered about their caretaker at the last, pulling him back from final Consumption, reshaping him even as the Void restored him to the semblance of life. A strange semblance, for the being called Tombs no longer resembles the man he once was, nor any other of Ainon's races. He is a thing of earth and stone and memory, a necropolis in miniature, animated by the echos of the grateful children. It is their love for him that strikes out at enemies during battle.

name: Voltz
title: The Enthusiast
bio: The spark of creativity is strong in the dwarves of the Crucible. In some, it could be said it courses perhaps a little too strongly. Voltz's mine overseer parents realized this was the case with their son very early on. Always eager to push the threshold of risk beyond prudence, the young dwarf was assigned to the repairs department of the artificer's guild and given his own workshop. There his fascination with the machines used to carve precious ore from their rocky environs could be put to good — and relatively harmless — use.

Voltz worked quickly, fixing more equipment than he blew up, and earned some autonomy — a rare commodity in the highly structured society of the Crucible. Autonomy meant free time, which he spent on his pet project: re-engineering mining machinery to discharge concentrated Crystalium energy. But his work began to worry other dwarfs, who feared he'd bring the mountain down on everyone's heads. Voltz itched for greater freedom to experiment with his still unstable designs.

When a mining team uncovered a Void lair in the mines, Hunters were contracted to clear it out. Voltz, acting without permission, joined them. He reveled in the chance to prove his admittedly wild imagination led to more than reckless endangerment. His Crystalium weapon proved itself highly effective as he took out more of the enemy even than the Hunters.

Voltzs furious parents threatened banishment, a frightening proposition for most dwarves. Voltz, though, had enticing opportunities above ground, for the Hunters had invited him to join their company.

name: Vukaru
title: The Shacklebreaker
bio: The frontier lands claimed by Vukaru's tribe were the first to be despoiled by the Archlanders' southward expansion. Vukaru's father, the chief, sought aid from neighbors to stand against the invaders, but years of bitter intertribal warfare meant there was no goodwill between them. With no help coming, Vukaru's father offered gifts and accommodation to appease the Archlander invaders.

Vukaru was furious at his father's lack of spine. Their line was descended from Crowncracker himself! Acting recklessly, Vukaru led the tribe's bravest against the intruders, but the humans somehow contrived to lure Consumed onto the battlefield.

Vukaru and a handful of braves survived thanks only to the arrival of his father and older brother with the rest of their warriors, but both the chief and his heir were lost. The chieftain's regalia fell to Vukaru.

Crushed and despondent by his defeat and the deaths of his father and brother, Vukaru thought himself unworthy of leadership and, even more so, of Crowncracker's legacy. Instead, he exiled himself, but he soon found himself pursued, then captured by slavers and forced to fight as a gladiator owned by one of the very chieftains who had refused to answer his father's call for aid.

Shame of being shackled burned in him, expressed in battle as a berserk rage. But even his fierce heart could not fuel such anger forever. Slowly, he learned to channel the fury. Ironically, it was the Void that set him free when it attacked his master's tribe. In the chaos, Vukaru escaped and, for the second time, witnessed the horror of the Void. This time he saw an entire tribe wiped out by the minions of the great Enemy. Free at last, he could have returned to his tribe, but he felt called to a different purpose. So, he journeyed into Archlands to seek allies against the Void.

name: Ygravain
title: The Inviolate
bio: Spare me, Light, and I swear I will be your steadfast servant. Thus prayed young Ygravain as the monster that had ransacked his home charged toward him. But the blow never fell. None other than Blasius Luca, the Fist of the Light, destroyed the Host with one swing of "Condemner." Ygravain could only weep. "Feel this pain tomorrow, boy", Luca said. "Now is the time for you to determine why you yet live." Inheriting his father's wealth, Ygravain dedicated himself to martial training. Whenever he faltered, he would remember Luca's lesson. Pain tomorrow; victory today.

He had always been a robust child, and training made him more so, but as time went on it seemed he was protected by some higher power. He seemed able to endure any hurt and to have inexhaustible vigor. His wounds healed quickly and rarely even scarred. It was the Light, he was sure, that interceded on his behalf. And he was not the only one to think so, for once he became a paladin — eventually the greatest among them save Luca himself — all the Legions praised Ygravain the Inviolate, the Light's unbreakable champion.

But Ygravain was breaking, tormented for years with nightmares of an armored figure, twisted and roaring at him in pain. The hierarchs told him it was only the Void's attention, a sign of his strength in the Light, but this offered no solace. He prayed with fanatical intensity, wore hair shirts, and sought out and faced the most powerful Hosts.

Nightmares plagued him still, and he begged the Light for death. Ashamed, Ygravain worried he was losing a spiritual battle, that he had lost the Light's favor. Finally, he undertook a quest to seek answers and maybe a fateful meeting with the being that hounded his restless sleep.

Ygravain gains 1 True to Vows each time a Holy Order ally grants Shield, Guard or Invulnerability to a Holy Order or Knight ally or 2 True to Vows if that ally is Holy Order Knight.

skills.name: VOID EMBRACE
skills.description:
Ygravain can trigger Embrace The Void to gain Void Power.
Ygravain gains +15% Defense, Crit Defense and Life Drain per 1 Void Power.

[On Embrace The Void] Ygravain gains 3 Wrath and 2 Guard.

[On Turn Start] Ygravain gains 1 Wrath per 1 Void Power.

When an enemy under Void Minion is defeated, Ygravain gains 2% Void Touch Cleanse (or 5% if they were defeated by Ygravain) (limit 1 per enemy).
